name: Teleport Blink
description: A short-range teleport that instantly relocates the player to a validated destination.
design_notes: Teleport blink removes travel time, so validation and communication matter more than acceleration. The player must understand range, blocked destinations, line of sight, recovery, and whether blink preserves momentum or resets it. A blink that lands inside geometry destroys trust immediately.
design_purpose: Give players a precise burst relocation option that bypasses ordinary pathing.
Create combat and traversal decisions around destination choice, cooldown, and recovery.
Support fantasy of slipping through danger without requiring physical travel across the intervening space.
player_fantasy: The player feels able to vanish from one spot and reappear exactly where they intended.

parameters.name: blink_max_distance
parameters.description: Maximum distance from origin to destination.

parameters.name: blink_cast_radius
parameters.description: Collision radius used when validating the teleport path or destination.

parameters.name: destination_clearance_height
parameters.description: Vertical clearance required at the arrival point.

parameters.name: ledge_snap_distance
parameters.description: Optional snap allowance to safe ledges or floor positions.

parameters.name: post_blink_recovery_ms
parameters.description: Control or attack recovery after arrival.

edge_cases: Destination validation must reject walls, thin ceilings, moving hazards, occupied spaces, and out-of-bounds voids.
Blinking across navmesh gaps should decide whether enemies can follow or retain targeting.
Momentum, projectiles, grapples, and status effects need rules for whether they persist through blink.
Networked blink should predict visual response but let the server validate final destination.
common_bugs: The destination marker is valid in the preview but invalid when the player confirms due to stale collision data.
Blink preserves downward velocity and instantly kills the player after arrival.
Arrival effects spawn at the origin because teleport state clears before presentation events read it.
Server correction rubber-bands the player back after a locally accepted blink.
balancing_notes: Long-range blink bypasses level geometry and enemy zoning; cooldown and destination validation become major balance levers.
If blink grants invulnerability or attack canceling, tune it as both traversal and defense.
accessibility_notes: Show a clear valid/invalid destination indicator using more than color, and allow confirm-to-blink for precision modes.
Avoid disorienting camera snaps; preserve facing or offer a predictable recenter option after teleport.

implementation_notes.unity: Preview with spherecasts or capsule checks, validate floor and head clearance, then move the CharacterController or Rigidbody through a teleport function that resets transient movement state.
implementation_notes.godot: Use physics direct space queries to test destination clearance, then set global_position only after clearing velocity, floor state, and collision recovery rules.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use capsule sweeps and navigation projection for destination validation, then call teleport or server-authoritative movement correction with replicated effects.
implementation_notes.web: Validate blink with swept collision or grid occupancy queries, commit origin and destination in one simulation event, and keep presentation trails separate from state.

example_games.title: Dishonored
example_games.note: Uses Blink as a short-range teleport for stealth and traversal.

example_games.title: Overwatch
example_games.note: Uses Tracer's Blink for rapid combat repositioning.
